entries.title: COMMENTARY ON PAUL'S EPISTLES on first-corinthians 9:3
entries.excerpt: Here Paul begins to develop the argument which he set out above, namely: "All things are lawful for me, but I will not be enslaved by anything" (6:12).

entries.title: Homily on 1 Corinthians 21 on first-corinthians 9:3
entries.excerpt:
"My defence to them that examine me is this."

What is, "My defence to them that examine me is this?" "To those who seek to know whereby I am proved to be an Apostle, or who accuse me as receiving money, or inquire the cause of my not receiving, or would fain shew that I am not an Apostle: to all such, my instruction given to you and these things which I am about to say, may stand for a full explanation and defence."

entries.title: COMMENTARY ON THE FIRST EPISTLE TO THE CORINTHIANS 219 on first-corinthians 9:3
entries.excerpt: Paul says that if anyone wants to examine his works, let him look at the Corinthians, for they are a sufficient witness to his labors.

entries.title: Commentary on 1 Corinthians on first-corinthians 9:3
entries.excerpt: To those wishing to know whence it is evident that I am an apostle, I will present you in my defense. For having proved that you were taught everything by me, I will strike down those who condemn me.
